It will be EXCLUDED from the model (not placed at a guessed center) and recorded as a Tab-3 model-derived assumption — add an X and/or Y location dimension on the drawing and re-run.

Hole position table has overlapping/conflicting dimension chains (2.250, 2.500±.005, 2.875, 5.375, 3.50, 1.156 TYP, 2.344 TYP) - some appear redundant or reference different features; positions for F005, F006, F007 are best-guess reconstructions from witness line tracing and may require human verification
M5x.8 TAP THRU note references '(2) HL'S (3) PL'S' which is ambiguous - interpreted as 3 total places with dimensions for spacing, but exact quantity/pattern intent should be confirmed
Only two orthographic views provided (front stepped profile and thin side edge); top/bottom/second_side views not available separately in this submission, limiting full 3D reconstruction confidence

overview_analysis.overall_shape_summary: The part is a flat, .25-thick 1018 steel plate with a stepped (non-rectangular) outline roughly 6.88 wide by 5.50 tall, featuring two .422 DIA through-holes and three M5x.8 tapped through-holes arranged at specific X/Y coordinate offsets rather than in a circular/polar pattern.

views_detected.description: Stepped-profile plate view showing a multi-tier outline with hole centerlines for two .422 DIA THRU holes and three M5x.8 TAP THRU holes, fully dimensioned in X (horizontal) offsets from a datum edge.
Narrow edge/profile view showing plate thickness of .25 and overall height of 5.50, with hidden lines indicating hole depth through the thin section.
Title block with material (1018 STEEL), heat treatment box, part name PLATE, drawing number M121-B, model number 250 BX, and standard tolerance/projection notes.

symmetry.notes: All hole locations are dimensioned using explicit X (horizontal, e.g. 1.88, 2.25, 2.50, 2.875, 4.38, 5.375, 6.88) and Y (vertical, e.g. .375, .938, 1.938, 2.500, 2.750, 2.938, 4.50) linear offsets from datum edges rather than polar/angular dimensioning. This rectangular coordinate dimensioning style is evidence AGAINST assuming any rotational (polar) hole pattern; the stepped, asymmetric profile of the plate outline also indicates no mirror symmetry about either axis.

flags_contributed.human_note: CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (front): Front view shows only 3 hole locations clearly circled/crossed with centerlines in the stepped body (2 at .422 DIA and what appears to be up to 3 M5x.8 TAP positions), but the note calls out '(2) HLS' for the .422 DIA and '(2) HLS. (3) PLS.' for the M5x.8 TAP, implying 2 tapped holes visible plus a 3rd 'PLS' (places) note that could mean 3 total places across the part -- the exact count and location of all 5 combined holes is not unambiguously resolved from the single front view alone since hole markers overlap with dimension leader lines. Recommendation: Verify against the actual hole pattern layout: count physical hole symbols (crossed circles) in the front view and match to 2x .422 DIA + 3x M5x.8 TAP = 5 total holes. Cross-check with an isometric or additional view if available, or confirm with the manufacturer that '3 PLS' means 3 locations for the M5x.8 tap.

CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (sheet): No top or plan view is provided showing hole positions in the other axis (depth into the part) -- only one primary front view with X-dimensioning is present, so the Y-axis (vertical) hole spacing is inferred from stacked vertical dimensions (4.50, 2.938, 2.750, 1.938, 2.500, .938 etc.) without a clear secondary confirming view. Recommendation: Request or verify a top/plan view or isometric to confirm vertical hole placement is consistent with the stepped profile edges shown in front view.

resolution.plain_english: F007 diameter inferred as 0.1969 inch from the thread callout (standard major diameter) — verify against the drawing. 1 of 22 dimension(s) required an engineering assumption (2 critical, 0 low, 1 medium). Model is complete and buildable as-is; verify flagged items in SolidWorks.
